options: Modern science emerged thanks to rapid scientific developments that occurred in Europe between roughly 1500 and 1750.

questions.text: Mühendislerden sadece işlerini yetkin bir şekilde yerine getirmeleri değil, aynı zamanda mühendisliğin ahlaki ve toplumsal etkilerinin de farkında olmaları beklenmektedir.
options: Engineers should not only perform their work competently, but consider almost all ethical and social aspects of their profession as well.

Engineers are expected not only to perform their work competently but also to be aware of the ethical and social effects of engineering.
questions.correct_answer: Engineers are expected not only to perform their work competently but also to be aware of the ethical and social effects of engineering.

questions.text: 9 Kızıl sincaplar, sonbaharda hatırlaması kolay yerlere gömdükleri yiyecek depolarına güvendikleri için kış uykusuna yatmazlar.

Red squirrels bury the caches of food in an easy-to-remember site in autumn in order not to hibernate.
Red squirrels cannot hibernate; as a result, they have to bury their food in easy-to-remember sites in autumn.
questions.correct_answer: As they rely on the caches of food they buried in easy-to-remember sites in autumn, red squirrels do not hibernate.

questions.text: Gerekli türbinlerin kurulum maliyeti, rüzgâr gücünün yaygın kullanımının önünde her zaman bir engel olmuştur.

The widespread use of wind power has slowed down because of the high installation cost of necessary turbines.

questions.text: Evlerde kömür kullanımı da azaltılmadıkça, Çin’in enerji santrallerinden karbon salınımını azaltma planları çok etkili olmayabilir.

China’s plans to reduce emissions from power plants may not be effective if the use of health-damaging coal is also reduced.
If household use of coal is also reduced, China’s plans to reduce emissions from power plants will have significant effects.
China’s plans to reduce harmful emissions from power plants will not be very effective if people keep using coal for heating.
As long as the household use of coal is not also reduced, China’s plans to reduce carbon emission from power plants may not be very effective.
questions.correct_answer: As long as the household use of coal is not also reduced, China’s plans to reduce carbon emission from power plants may not be very effective.

questions.text: 2017 yılında yayımlanan bir rapora göre, Avrupa’daki kirlenmiş toprak miktarının 2025 yılına kadar %50 artması beklenmektedir.

questions.text: Concorde, the fastest passenger aircraft of its time, cruised at speeds of over 2,180 kilometres per hour – twice the speed of sound. At half that speed, it would break the sound barrier, generating an enormous double sonic boom that could be heard for miles. This incredibly loud noise led to a worldwide ban on continental supersonic flights. -------. To achieve this, it has announced plans to develop a 'low boom' aircraft, which generates a soft thump as it breaks the sound barrier, rather than a disruptive boom.
options: Scientists used to work on a project to stop shockwaves generated by supersonic aircrafts
Shockwaves move away from the aircraft in all directions and travel at the speed of sound
In 2016, a group of engineers wanted to start a new spacecraft project, but they could not afford it
Now, NASA hopes to bring back supersonic passenger air travel by making flights greener, safer and quieter
These factors contributed to the aircraft’s unexpected worldwide failure at the beginning of 2000
questions.correct_answer: Now, NASA hopes to bring back supersonic passenger air travel by making flights greener, safer and quieter

questions.text: The oyster has a hard outer wall known as a mantle, which covers and protects certain organs. When an intruder such as a grain of sand enters an oyster’s shell, it irritates the oyster. Therefore, the oyster promptly covers the foreign object with a mineral substance called nacre. The oyster coats it with more and more nacre until it forms a pearl. -------. Pearl 'farmers' can harvest pearls by triggering this response by inserting a grain of sand into the shell.
options: But oysters can produce pearls in a very short period of time
For example, producing pearls in this way helps them to expand their life spans
Recently, biologists have found that oysters produce nacre from their shells
So, the ecosystem where species like oysters live is still in danger of being polluted
The shiny spheres are thus actually the product of an immune response
questions.correct_answer: The shiny spheres are thus actually the product of an immune response

questions.text: 1 The field of electric power is concerned with the design and operation of systems for generating, transmitting, and distributing electric power. -------. Throughout history, man-made objects have played an important part in civilizations

questions.text: Sound is stored in one of two basic ways, called formats. The first format stores sound in a form that resembles the original sound wave and is known as analogue recording. -------. The second format is digital recording which represents a sound wave as a series of numbers. Computer drives use the position of magnetic particles to define these numbers. A number of digital formats exist, and they determine the quality of the recorded sound, and the number of channels of sound playback.
options: The term 'format' is also used to describe the number of channels, or streams of sound
This format is called analogue because the form of the sound wave in the recording is analogous to the original waveform
Two of the most common formats are monaural, or mono, and stereophonic, or stereo
When they are played, the sounds from these two separate channels combine in the air
They were really popular in the 1970s, mainly because they were easy to access then
questions.correct_answer: This format is called analogue because the form of the sound wave in the recording is analogous to the original waveform

questions.text: 4 66 - 68. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Since its construction, at least 1300 people have killed themselves by jumping off the Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco. In 1940, the Board of Directors discussed an “anti-suicide screen” but decided against it. In 2008, they voted 14 to 1 to install a stainless steel net but this time serious public disagreement led to a cancellation in construction. While the proponents of the project tended to disregard aesthetic considerations, those against the barrier believed the bridge had always been praised for its transparency and any barrier design would destroy the view. In addition, opponents claimed a suicide barrier would not be effective, since people who wanted to kill themselves would simply go somewhere else. However, those favouring the project claimed most suicidal people act on an impulse and, when prevented from actually killing themselves, often seek help instead of ending their lives. When it came to financial concerns, the barrier did not seem worth the costs to the opponents, whereas the proponents reacted by saying the value of a human life cannot be expressed in monetary terms. It is clear from the text that -------.
options: state authorities agreed to install anti-suicide netting but they could not afford it
there has been no agreement on the best way to address suicide attempts at the Golden Gate Bridge
the bridge is under construction now despite some opponents
a great number of people had been prevented from committing suicide by 2008
the authorities inspected some other bridges with similar designs before reaching a verdict
questions.correct_answer: there has been no agreement on the best way to address suicide attempts at the Golden Gate Bridge

questions.text: According to the text, some people were against the installation of a barrier since -------.
options: the project required structural change in the bridge and brought additional cost
suicide from the bridge was neither possible nor probable as the bridge was difficult to climb
it would prevent the public from noticing people who were about to commit suicide
people who were stopped when they were about to end their lives would usually seek psychological help
they believed people had free will and one should not interfere with people’s decisions to end their own lives
questions.correct_answer: the project required structural change in the bridge and brought additional cost

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: contradictory viewpoints regarding the Golden Gate Bridge suicide barrier
an inquiry into the pros and cons of the construction of the Golden Gate Bridge
cost-benefit and risk analysis prior to the construction of a bridge in San Francisco
the cost of redesigning bridges for public use in the city of San Francisco
social and ethical issues that arose during construction of the Golden Gate Bridge
questions.correct_answer: contradictory viewpoints regarding the Golden Gate Bridge suicide barrier

questions.text: 5 69 - 71. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. An increase in temperature of a few degrees might not sound so bad, but small changes in average temperature can lead to much more dramatic changes in climate patterns. These changes will cause some regions to warm much more than the average, while other regions may cool. Polar regions will warm the most, causing polar ice to melt. This is clearly threatening to the species of these regions, but it also warms the oceans everywhere and changes their salt content as melting ice pours fresh water into the sea. The fact that the waters of the Gulf of Mexico are at their warmest level in at least a century may be contributing to the greater strength of hurricanes that have recently blown out of the Caribbean. More generally, overall warming of the atmosphere will increase evaporation from the oceans, leading to numerous and intense storms; ironically, this means that global warming could mean more severe winter blizzards. It is clear from the text that -------.
options: because of the change in temperature, most areas will become desert-like
low temperature is a sign of disasters such as hurricanes
some areas are warmer because of the increase in the salt level in seas
even the slightest changes in temperature can affect climate patterns
a few degrees’ increase in temperature is better than a decrease
questions.correct_answer: even the slightest changes in temperature can affect climate patterns

questions.text: It is stated in the text that the reason for the change in the salt content of seas is that -------.
options: more fresh water will flow into the seas due to melting polar ice
the water in seas will evaporate, causing storms and hurricanes
some regions like the Caribbean are likely to get much more rain
endangered species in polar regions will become extinct
climate patterns across the world may cause certain regions to get cooler
questions.correct_answer: more fresh water will flow into the seas due to melting polar ice

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: to what extent increases in temperature are affecting marine life
how the Gulf of Mexico is affected by an increase in temperature
climate change and its influence on animals in polar regions
how we can help protect the environment
the consequences of changes in temperatures across the world
questions.correct_answer: the consequences of changes in temperatures across the world

questions.text: 6 72 - 74. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Driving is a much more complex activity than most people think. It involves a wide range of skills and actions. Some of them are easier to automate than others. Maintaining speed on an open road is simple, which is why standard cruise control systems have been doing it automatically for a long time. Besides lane-keeping systems, adaptive cruise control systems that keep proper speed and spacing behind other vehicles are also widely available. However, it will be an enormous jump from such systems to fully automated driving, which is still something we can only have in science-fiction films. The automotive industry and the media have made a mess of the terminology used to talk about automated driverless systems. The terms 'autonomous', 'driverless' and 'self-driving' confuse more than they enlighten. To clear things up, scientists have defined different levels of automation. They arranged them on a ladder of decreasing dependence on the driver. According to the text, the technical terms used by automotive manufacturers and the press -------.
options: set an important safety target for automated driving systems
cause confusion among the public about automated driverless systems
sound futuristic, like having your own personal electronic chauffeur
will bring automated valet-parking systems in the near future
are only related to autopilot in speciﬁc scenarios, such as freeway traffic jams
questions.correct_answer: cause confusion among the public about automated driverless systems

few people think driving is an easy task to execute
there is still a long way to go before we can switch to fully automated cars
the production of smart cars will have to end before long
the safety of modern car systems is still under debate
questions.correct_answer: there is still a long way to go before we can switch to fully automated cars

steering control must be affordable for the general public

questions.correct_answer: cruise control systems have been used for many years

questions.text: 7 75 - 77. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. The ozone layer protects life on Earth, and without ozone, the world as we know it would not exist. Ozone is a key element of the atmosphere, the blanket of gases surrounding our planet that is made up of discernable layers, which differ in temperature, density, and gas composition. The lowest level, the troposphere, extends about 11 km up from Earth’s surface. This level is familiar to us: it is the air we breathe and where our weather occurs. The next level in the atmospheric blanket, the stratosphere, rises to 50 km above Earth’s surface. The stratosphere is much less dense than the troposphere but contains a 'layer' of ozone – abbreviated as O3 because it contains three oxygen atoms – a region where most of the atmosphere’s ozone is found. It is clear from the text that -------.
options: the stratosphere has the least ozone compared to the other layers
the stratosphere is much closer to the Earth than the troposphere
the gases surrounding the Earth vary in density and content
the layers that make up the ozone layer have not been fully observed
all the layers in the atmosphere have almost the same features
questions.correct_answer: the gases surrounding the Earth vary in density and content

questions.text: According to the text, one of the reasons we know the troposphere well is that it -------.
options: contains the most vital ozone type
protects life on Earth better than the other layers
extends about 50 km up from Earth’s surface
is much thicker than the other layers
contains the air we inhale and exhale
questions.correct_answer: contains the air we inhale and exhale

questions.text: We can infer from the text that-------.
options: the ozone layer is a single body with no discernible layers
the ozone layer makes the world habitable for every living species
weather phenomena occur in the highest level of the atmosphere
the density of the ozone layer changes seasonally
there are two atmospheric layers between the Earth and the troposphere
questions.correct_answer: the ozone layer makes the world habitable for every living species

questions.text: 8 78 - 80. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Dreams have long been regarded as prophetic communications which enable us to foretell the future. There is absolutely no scientific evidence for this theory and considerable scientific evidence against it. It is certainly true that individuals who are concerned about a traumatic event, such as the threat of the loss of a loved one who is sick, will dream about that loved one more often. We know from our experiments that it is possible to influence dream content simply by having a subject in mind while going to sleep. Therefore, dreaming about a loved one at a time when that loved one’s life is threatened is not surprising. If the dreamer then calls and finds that the individual has died, it is understandable for him or her to assume that the dream was an early warning of that death. But this is a mistake. It is simply a coincidental correspondence between a situation about which one has legitimate and intense concern and the occurrence of the event that one fears. The text is mainly about -------.
options: the significance of dreams in ancient cultures
why people frequently dream of their loved ones
the association between dreams and reality
how we can control our dreams to know about the future
a proven theory about dreaming and its effects
questions.correct_answer: the association between dreams and reality

questions.text: According to the text, -------.
options: the form and content of our dreams are inseparable
science can help prove the prophetic quality of dreaming
our daily routine has no significant effect on the content of dreams
concerns about traumatic events may determine the content of our dreams
modern science has never been interested in content of dreams
questions.correct_answer: concerns about traumatic events may determine the content of our dreams

questions.text: From the text, we can clearly understand that -------.
options: most of our dreams tell us that we will lose one of our loved ones soon
people who have never had sleep problems can have lucid dreams
some incidents may make people think dreams are indicators of a future event
most people can remember their dreams quite easily
there is a certain relation between mental health and dreaming
questions.correct_answer: some incidents may make people think dreams are indicators of a future event
